notable_excerpts: Our renewable energy capacity stands at 292 MW powered by solar, wind and hydel resources.
We are on track to add another 230 MW in the coming quarters.
Our aluminium-specific greenhouse gas footprint for the quarter was 19.47 tons of CO2 per ton of aluminium produced.
We have created a green wall at Aditya by planting 1,500 fully grown trees to reduce dust emissions.
In this quarter, 78% of the total waste generated was recycled or reused.
Safety is always our highest priority, and our LTIFR for this quarter stands at 0.27.
We have now told every unit head to serve as a night duty officer on the shop floor once every 10 days.
We remain deeply committed to preserving and enhancing our biodiversity in and around the areas of operation.
We have with us on this call from Hindalco's management, Mr. Satish Pai, Managing Director; Mr. Bharat Goenka, Chief Financial Officer.
Following this presentation, the forum will be open for questions and answers.
We have been doing a return of capital from Novelis to Hindalco many times without any tax leakage.

chain_of_thought: Hindalco Industries demonstrates strong environmental initiatives with significant renewable energy projects and biodiversity efforts. Social aspects are well-addressed with a focus on safety and community engagement. Governance practices show transparency and risk management, though there is room for improvement in board independence and ethics disclosures.
strengths: Strong commitment to renewable energy and carbon reduction.
Comprehensive biodiversity and waste management initiatives.
Focus on employee safety and community engagement.
weaknesses: Limited disclosure on board independence and ethics.
Need for more detailed governance practices.
